item.title: Chiefs Recall 7 All Blacks for Reds Qualifying Final
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 06/06/2026 Chiefs coach Jono Gibbes has recalled seven All Blacks to his starting XV for tonight's Qualifying Final against the Reds in Hamilton.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 06/06/2026

Chiefs coach Jono Gibbes has recalled seven All Blacks to his starting XV for tonight's Qualifying Final against the Reds in Hamilton.

He has also moved Samipeni Finau to the bench and brought Leroy Carter back from injury via the reserves.

Damian McKenzie will start at first five after missing the last two games due to concussion.

He will have the in-form Quinn Tupaea outside him and the dynamic Isaac Hutchinson at fullback.

Luke Jacobson returns to captain the side from openside flanker and is joined by a host of All Blacks, with Ollie Norris, Samisoni Taukei'aho, Tupou Vaa'i and Simon Parker all returning to the starting lineup.

It means Sione Ahio is the only non-All Black in the forward pack, with two-thirds of the starting XV and half of the bench having worn the black jersey.

McKenzie is symptom-free and looking forward to returning to action after witnessing a strong performance against the Blues last week.

The Gallagher Chiefs have won 10 of their last 13 Shop N Save Super Rugby Pacific matches against the Reds, including their last three in a row. This will be the third time the two sides have met in the finals, with the Chiefs winning both previous encounters.

The Chiefs have also won 10 of their last 12 finals matches at FMG Stadium Waikato, including all six of their playoff games against Australian opposition.

The Chiefs will take on the Reds at 7.05 tonight.

item.title: Bula Boys suffer 2-1 defeat in friendly match
item.description: Bula Boys suffer 2-1 defeat in friendly match

item.title: Labasa town gears up for Big Fiji FACT as preparations intensify
item.description: Labasa town is gearing up for a busy week of football as preparations intensify for the 2026 Big Fiji FACT tournament at Subrail Park. As excitement builds, Labasa Town Administrator Paul Jadu Ram says the town is expected to see a significant increase in activity during the tournament, with teams, officials and supporters travelling to Labasa for the four-day event.
item.content:encoded:
Labasa town is gearing up for a busy week of football as preparations intensify for the 2026 Big Fiji FACT tournament at Subrail Park.

As excitement builds, Labasa Town Administrator Paul Jadu Ram says the town is expected to see a significant increase in activity during the tournament, with teams, officials and supporters travelling to Labasa for the four-day event.

Ram is urging fans to leave home early to avoid traffic congestion around Subrail Park and is also advising visitors from outside Labasa to secure accommodation as soon as possible.

He says the Labasa Town Council is working to ensure the venue is ready before the tournament begins, with improvement works currently underway, including the painting of the pavilion to enhance the viewing experience for spectators.

Ram is also calling on Labasa supporters to come out in numbers and rally behind the home team throughout the competition.

He says all preparations are on track to be completed before the opening day of the tournament.

The 2026 Big Fiji FACT will be held at Subrail Park from 18th to 21st June, with fans expected to witness four days of top football action as teams compete for one of the country's most prestigious titles.

The semi-finals will be held on 27th June, with the grand final scheduled for 28th June at Subrail Park.

item.title: RKS crush Sila Central 46-3
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 06/06/2026 Defending Under-18 Vodafone Secondary Schools Rugby champions Ratu Kadavulevu School kicked off their Eastern Zone campaign in emphatic fashion, thrashing Sila Central High School 46-3.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 06/06/2026

Defending Under-18 Vodafone Secondary Schools Rugby champions Ratu Kadavulevu School kicked off their Eastern Zone campaign in emphatic fashion, thrashing Sila Central High School 46-3.

The boys from Delana Kaikai have sent an early warning to the rest of the competition as they begin their defence of the prestigious title.

In other Under-18 results, Lelean Memorial School defeated Queen Victoria School 26-10.

Meanwhile, Ratu Kadavulevu School beat Tailevu North College 23-11 in the U17 grade.

In the southern zone competition, Nasinu Secondary School thrashed Church College Fiji 51-3 in the U18 grade.

item.title: Babasiga Lions eye sixth Fiji FACT title as defence begins at home
item.description: As defending Fiji FACT champions, Extra Supermarket Labasa FC will once again look to draw inspiration from their proud history that has seen the Babasiga Lions lift the coveted trophy five times. Labasa's name is deeply woven into the history of the Fiji FACT competition, with the northern giants establishing themselves as one of the tournament's most successful and passionate teams.
item.content:encoded:
As defending Fiji FACT champions, Extra Supermarket Labasa FC will once again look to draw inspiration from their proud history that has seen the Babasiga Lions lift the coveted trophy five times.

Labasa's name is deeply woven into the history of the Fiji FACT competition, with the northern giants establishing themselves as one of the tournament's most successful and passionate teams.

With five Fiji FACT titles and nine runner-up finishes, the Babasiga Lions have built a legacy fuelled by strong northern support, exciting football and memorable tournament moments.

The side first tasted Fiji FACT success in 1992 when they defeated Nadroga 1-0 at Subrail Park in front of a massive home crowd. The victory remains one of the proudest moments in Labasa football history and marked the last time the district won the tournament on home soil.

Labasa added another chapter to its success story in 1997 when they were declared joint winners alongside Ba after the final ended in a scoreless draw.

Two years later, the Babasiga Lions secured their third Fiji FACT title with a thrilling 2-1 victory over Lautoka in the 1999 final.

After a 16-year title drought, Labasa returned to the top in 2015, defeating Rewa 2-0 in Nadi to end their long wait and spark celebrations across the North.

The team's most recent triumph came in 2025 when they overcame Rewa in a dramatic final at the HFC Bank Stadium in Suva. The match ended in a 1-all draw after full-time before remaining scoreless in extra time, with Labasa prevailing 3-1 in the penalty shootout to claim a 4-2 overall victory.

While the Babasiga Lions have also suffered heartbreak in several Fiji FACT finals against teams including Ba, Suva, Rewa and Nadroga, their resilience and consistency have made them one of the competition's most feared teams.

With another Fiji FACT campaign on the horizon, Labasa will be hoping to add another title to its illustrious history and further cement its place among Fiji football's elite clubs.

The Babasiga Lions will begin their campaign at Subrail Park against Suva on 19th June at 7.30pm.

The team will then face Navua on 20th June at 6pm before taking on rivals Rewa at 3pm on 21st June.

Meanwhile, the pool matches will be played from 19th to 21st June before the action returns for the semi-finals on 27th June and the grand final on 28th June at Subrail Park.

item.description: Luke Jacobson was “frustrated” as a combination of bad luck and being overlooked denied him any playing time in the All Blacks’ tr
